grants.description: Themed S-Process grant round positioning human enhancement and empowerment as a hedge against extinction risk from advanced AI. Funds organizations working on AI-relevant cognitive/coordination enhancement and longterm flourishing.

grants.name: AI Risk Mitigation Fund
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling; spun out of LTFF in Dec 2023 with dedicated focus on catastrophic AI risk.
grants.description: Grants to technical AI alignment research (interpretability, oversight, dangerous-capability evals), AI policy work on catastrophic risks, and training programs to bring new researchers into AI safety. 100% of donations passed through to grantees.

grants.name: RAND Technology and Security Policy (TASP) Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling applications. Requires ability to obtain US or UK government security clearance.
grants.duration: 6 months, renewable up to 3 years; full- or part-time
grants.description: Fellows conduct policy research at RAND on AI security, frontier AI risks, biosecurity, and other emerging technologies, with mentorship from RAND policy experts. Open from undergraduate through mid-career.

grants.name: Wellcome Leap Focused Antibiotics
grants.deadlineNote: Full proposal portal opens June 22, 2026; closes June 29, 2026 at 11:59pm ET. Decisions July 29, 2026.

grants.description: USD 50M Wellcome Leap program to develop reformulated, gut-sparing antibiotics that treat infections while preserving the gut microbiome. Open to universities, companies, and non-profits worldwide; abstract round preceded the full proposal window.

grants.name: MJFF Parkinson's Disease Proteoforms Program

grants.name: MJFF GBA1 Parkinson's Disease Research Catalyst Program

grants.name: Susan G. Komen Career Catalyst Research (CCR) Grants
grants.deadlineNote: Institutional opt-in by June 10, 2026; LOI nominations by July 29, 2026; full applications due Oct 5, 2026. Limited submission - max two nominees per institution.

grants.name: BrightFocus Alzheimer's Disease Research Award

grants.description: Supports innovative Alzheimer's and related dementia research. Standard Awards target early-career investigators within 15 years of terminal degree; Postdoctoral Fellowships within 5 years. Researchers from AI, engineering, ML, physics, and cancer biology are encouraged to apply.

grants.name: Burroughs Wellcome Fund Career Awards at the Scientific Interface (CASI)
grants.deadlineNote: LOI due August 14, 2026, 3pm EDT. Full application January 11, 2027 (by invitation).
grants.description: Bridges postdoctoral training and the first three years of faculty service for researchers from mathematics, physics, chemistry, CS, statistics, or engineering moving into biology. Requires 12-60 months postdoc experience and no tenure-track appointment yet.

grants.name: Cystic Fibrosis Foundation Clinical Research Award (Fall 2026)
grants.deadlineNote: Fall 2026 LOI was April 2, 2026; full applications due Aug 17, 2026 by 5pm ET.
grants.description: Supports clinical research in cystic fibrosis spanning epidemiology, therapeutics, behavioral interventions, and care delivery. Applications submitted via awards.cff.org.

grants.name: DBT-BIRAC Bio-AI for High-Performance Biomanufacturing (BioE3)
grants.funder: Department of Biotechnology (DBT) and BIRAC, Government of India

grants.description: Joint DBT-BIRAC call under the BioE3 Policy to integrate AI with biotechnology across five themes - biomolecular design, sustainable agriculture, synthetic biology, Ayurveda, and genome diagnostics - supporting hubs for high-performance biomanufacturing.

grants.name: Grand Challenges India - Cost-Disruptive Screening and Diagnostic Innovations

grants.description: Open call for indigenous, cost-disruptive screening and diagnostic innovations targeting tuberculosis, emerging pathogens, syndromic testing, and enteric pathogens - point-of-care and ultra-low-cost solutions from Indian startups, industry, and academia.

grants.name: Fogarty Emerging Global Leader Award (K43)

grants.description: International research career development award for early-career investigators from low- and middle-income countries to lead a focused biomedical/behavioral research project with protected research time at an LMIC institution.

grants.name: DAAD Research Grants - Doctoral Programmes / Short-Term and One-Year Grants
grants.deadlineNote: Research Grants - Short-Term and One-Year: 7 Oct 2026. Doctoral Programmes (incl. Bi-nationally Supervised/Cotutelle) for funding starting Oct 2027: 21 Oct 2026.
grants.amount: EUR 934/month (doctoral); EUR 1,300/month (postdoc); plus travel, insurance, research allowance

grants.description: Bilateral mobility grants for Indian doctoral candidates and early-career postdocs to conduct research stays in Germany, including bi-nationally supervised PhD (Cotutelle) arrangements with German hosts.

grants.description: Open to researchers of all nationalities, including India. Postdocs must be within 4 years of PhD; experienced researchers within 12 years. Applicant identifies an academic host in Germany.

grants.name: JSPS Postdoctoral Fellowship for Research in Japan (Standard)

grants.description: For early-career postdoctoral researchers from any country outside the UK (incl. India and LMICs) with PhD plus up to ~5 years experience, to hold a fellowship at a UK host institution.

grants.description: Travel/subsistence funding for UK-based researchers to build collaborations with overseas partners, including a dedicated India stream (Yusuf and Farida Hamied Foundation) for collaborations with Indian researchers.

grants.name: Fulbright-Nehru Postdoctoral Research Fellowship

grants.description: For Indian postdoctoral researchers (PhD/DM awarded between Jul 2022 and Jul 2026) to undertake research at a US host institution. Government of India / state civil servants ineligible; CSIR, ICAR, DRDO, ISRO, ICMR, IISc, IIT researchers eligible.

grants.name: Fulbright-Nehru Academic and Professional Excellence Fellowships

grants.description: For mid-career to senior Indian faculty and professionals in STEM and other fields to undertake teaching, research, or combined assignments at US universities. Indian citizenship required.

grants.name: Raman-Charpak Fellowship

grants.name: Erasmus+ International Credit Mobility (KA171)

grants.name: AMS Centennial Research Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: 2027-2028 cycle applications open Jul 15 - Sep 30, 2026
grants.description: Annual fellowship for outstanding early-career mathematicians (3-12 years post-PhD) at North American institutions to support a year of dedicated research.

grants.name: ICTP Mathematics Postdoctoral Fellowships

grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowships at ICTP's Mathematics section for young mathematicians with strong research records. Preference given to candidates from developing countries who will engage with ICTP's mission.

grants.name: Banff International Research Station (BIRS) Workshop Proposals
grants.deadlineNote: Annual call (2028 program) expected around Sep 2026; 2027 call closed Sep 2025

grants.name: Oberwolfach Research Fellows (OWRF / Research in Pairs)

grants.description: Successor to Research in Pairs. Supports small collaborative groups (2-4 researchers) in residence at MFO for 1-4 weeks of focused mathematical research; Leibniz Fellowships allow single-researcher stays.

grants.name: Fields Institute Postdoctoral Fellowships

grants.deadlineNote: Tied to specific thematic programs; deadlines vary, posted on MathJobs

grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowships tied to Fields Institute thematic programs in pure and applied mathematics, including upcoming programs in quantum algorithms and mathematics for AI safety. Usually co-funded with a sponsoring university.

grants.name: Hausdorff Research Institute (HIM) Trimester Program Participation

grants.name: Erdős Center Semester Postdoctoral Fellowships

grants.description: Postdoctoral positions linked to Erdős Center thematic semesters in areas including algebraic geometry, harmonic analysis, number theory, Ramsey theory, complex geometry, automorphic forms, and large networks. No teaching duties.

grants.name: IAS/Park City Mathematics Institute (PCMI) Summer Programs
grants.deadlineNote: Annual mid-January deadlines; 2027 cycle expected ~Jan 15-31, 2027
grants.amount: Lodging, meals, partial travel; stipend support varies by program

grants.name: Heilbronn Institute Small Grants and International Visitors Scheme
grants.deadlineNote: Small Grants reviewed on a rolling basis; Focused Research Grants annually in November

Includes Small Grants, Focused Research Grants, and an International Visitors Scheme for hosting overseas experts.

grants.name: 51 Pegasi b Fellowship

grants.name: KITP Postdoctoral Scholars in Theoretical Physics

grants.name: Kavli IPMU Postdoctoral Fellowships
grants.funder: Kavli Institute for the Physics and Mathematics of the Universe, University of Tokyo
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; recent calls have closed in late November / early December for September starts. The 2027 main call is expected to open in autumn 2026 via Academic Jobs Online.
grants.description: Postdoctoral positions spanning astrophysics, cosmology, particle physics, mathematics and statistics at Kavli IPMU in Kashiwa, Japan, with research allowance and ability to work abroad up to three months per year.

grants.name: ESO Fellowship Programme
grants.deadlineNote: Annual deadline of 15 October. Applicants need a PhD in astronomy, physics or a related discipline by ~November of the start year.
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowships at ESO Headquarters (Garching, Germany) or in Chile, combining independent research with functional duties supporting ESO observatories and instruments.

grants.name: NASA Hubble Fellowship Program (Hubble/Einstein/Sagan)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; the 2027 call is expected to open September 2026 with a late-October 2026 deadline (mirroring the 2026 cycle, which closed October 29, 2025).
grants.description: Prestigious umbrella postdoctoral fellowship covering NASA astrophysics: Hubble (cosmic origins), Einstein (physics of the cosmos), and Sagan (exoplanets). Salary plus benefits, travel, and research allowance at chosen US host institutions.

grants.name: IUCAA Postdoctoral Fellowships (incl. Vaidya-Raychaudhury)
grants.funder: Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA), Pune
grants.deadlineNote: Next call deadline 5 July 2026. Up to eight positions awarded annually across regular and named fellowships in astronomy and astrophysics.
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowships at IUCAA, Pune, covering theoretical, observational, and instrumentation research in astronomy and astrophysics, with on-campus housing and contingency funding.

grants.name: Aspen Center for Physics Summer Workshop Participation
grants.deadlineNote: Annual summer workshop participation; application window closes 15 January for the following summer (e.g., 2027 summer cycle opens fall 2026 with 15 January 2027 deadline). PhD required (or expected in program year). Minimum 2-week stay; limited financial support for early-career and underrepresented participants.
grants.description: Participation in self-organized summer workshops in theoretical physics, astrophysics, cosmology, condensed matter, and biological physics at Aspen, Colorado. Open by application; postdocs may apply for travel/lodging support.

grants.name: Center for Astrophysics | SAO Predoctoral Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Two cycles per year: May 1 and November 1. Open to PhD students enrolled at a university worldwide who wish to do part or all of their thesis research under SAO scientists.
grants.description: Predoctoral fellowship for PhD students to carry out thesis research at the Smithsonian Astrophysical Observatory in Cambridge, MA, under the supervision of a CfA scientist.

grants.name: ARIES Postdoctoral Fellowship
grants.funder: Aryabhatta Research Institute of Observational Sciences (ARIES), Nainital
grants.deadlineNote: Calls advertised periodically through the year; current RA/PDF/SPA call closes 15 June 2026. Open to candidates with a PhD in astronomy, astrophysics, or atmospheric science.
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowships at ARIES, Nainital, with research in observational astronomy, solar physics, stellar physics, galaxies, and atmospheric science, including access to the 3.6 m Devasthal Optical Telescope.

grants.name: Camille Dreyfus Teacher-Scholar Awards
grants.deadlineNote: Annual call; 2026 deadline (Feb 3) has passed. 2027 call expected to open late 2026 with February 2027 deadline.
grants.description: Early-career chemistry, biochemistry, materials chemistry, or chemical engineering faculty at PhD-granting institutions within their first six years of independent academic appointment. One nomination per institution; recognizes outstanding scholarship combined with commitment to education.

grants.name: Henry Dreyfus Teacher-Scholar Awards
grants.amount: $75,000 unrestricted research grant (includes $5,000 for departmental expenses)
grants.description: Chemistry faculty at primarily undergraduate institutions (no doctoral program) between their 4th and 12th years of independent academic careers, conducting research substantially with undergraduates. One nomination per institution.

grants.name: Welch Foundation Research Grants
grants.deadlineNote: Applications accepted Nov 1 - Jan 31 annually; portal opens via ProposalCentral.
grants.description: Supports basic chemistry research by tenured or tenure-track faculty at Texas universities/colleges listed by the Texas Higher Education Coordinating Board. Funds may cover personnel, equipment, supplies, publication, and travel.

grants.name: ACS PRF New Directions (ND) Grants
grants.funder: American Chemical Society Petroleum Research Fund
grants.deadlineNote: Fall 2026 submission window: Sep 14 - Oct 9, 2026. Spring 2027 window: Feb 15 - Mar 12, 2027.
grants.description: Supports faculty at PhD-granting institutions to pursue a new research direction in fundamental science related to petroleum or fossil fuels, distinct from currently funded work. ACS membership not required.

grants.name: ACS PRF Doctoral New Investigator (DNI) Grants
grants.funder: American Chemical Society Petroleum Research Fund
grants.deadlineNote: Fall 2026 submission window: Sep 14 - Oct 9, 2026.
grants.description: Start-up funding for new tenure-track faculty at PhD-granting institutions in fields fundamental to petroleum or fossil fuel science. Applicant must be within first three years of first academic appointment.

grants.name: MRS Postdoctoral Award
grants.deadlineNote: Nominations accepted June 1 - August 1 annually for the following Spring Meeting recognition.
grants.amount: $3,000 cash prize plus travel reimbursement to MRS Spring Meeting
grants.description: Recognizes postdoctoral scholars showing exceptional promise across any class of materials research. Open to nominations regardless of nationality; preference for postdocs still in position at the recognition meeting.

grants.name: Royal Society of Chemistry Research Fund
grants.deadlineNote: Round 2 closes Nov 30, 2026; Round 1 (closed Jun 15, 2026) decisions early Sep 2026.
grants.description: Supports RSC members (Associate, Member, or Fellow) with limited access to research funds to start new chemistry projects. Prioritises researchers in developing countries, those at less well-funded institutes, and early-career investigators.

grants.name: RSC Researcher Development and Travel Grant
grants.deadlineNote: Multiple 2026 rounds remain: Round 3 closes Aug 3, 2026 (activities Oct 1, 2026+); Round 4 closes Oct 19, 2026 (activities Jan 1, 2027+). One grant per applicant every three years.
grants.description: Supports PhD students and early-career chemists (academia or industry) to attend conferences, training courses, or organise scientific meetings. One award per applicant every three years.

grants.name: GDCh Conference Scholarships (travel grants)
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling; tied to each conference's registration/presentation deadline. Over 1,000 travel grants awarded yearly.
grants.amount: Up to ~600 EUR (varies by venue: ~400 EUR national, ~500 EUR Europe, ~600 EUR outside Europe)
grants.description: Supports GDCh members (bachelor/master/doctoral students and trainees) to actively participate (lecture or poster) in national and international chemistry conferences. Karl Ziegler Foundation funds parallel travel grants for EuChemS and GDCh scientific forums.

grants.name: JST ERATO (Exploratory Research for Advanced Technology)
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling nominations year-round; annual cutoff (typically end of September JST) determines selection year cohort.
grants.region: Japan (Research Director must be affiliated with a domestic Japanese institution; nationality not a barrier)
grants.amount: Up to JPY 1.2 billion per project
grants.description: Funds high-risk, paradigm-shifting basic research led by an outstanding Research Director who assembles a project team. Strong fit for chemistry, materials, and interdisciplinary science. Email nominations to erato-suisen@jst.go.jp.

grants.name: Two Sigma PhD Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle opens mid-August and closes late September; the 2026 round closed 26 Sep 2025 and the 2027 round is expected to open around August 2026. Fellowship begins Fall of award year for PhD students in their 3rd year or later with 2+ years remaining.
grants.amount: Up to $75,000/year for two academic years (paid to university) + $10,000 one-time to the recipient
grants.description: Fellowship for US-based PhD students (3rd year+) in applied mathematics, computer science, statistics, finance/econometrics, or physics. Includes mentorship from Two Sigma researchers.

grants.name: Bloomberg Data Science PhD Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: 2026-2027 cycle closed 14 Dec 2025; next call (2027-2028) historically opens in November with mid-December deadline.
grants.region: Global (internship in NY, London, or Toronto)
grants.amount: 100% tuition + $45,000 annual stipend; paid summer internship
grants.description: Supports full-time PhD students in data science, machine learning, NLP, information retrieval, and related areas. Requires a 14-week summer internship at Bloomberg for each year of the fellowship.

grants.name: Hertz Foundation Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: 2027 application opens late August 2026 and closes late October 2026; reference deadline ~early November 2026.
grants.amount: Up to $250,000 over five years (stipend plus tuition equivalent)
grants.description: Highly selective PhD fellowship for applied physical, biological, and engineering sciences including computer science and AI. Fellows have unusual freedom to pursue high-risk research directions.

grants.name: National Defense Science and Engineering Graduate (NDSEG) Fellowship
grants.funder: U.S. Department of Defense (Air Force, Army, Navy)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle opens 15 Aug and closes mid-November; the FY2027 cycle is expected to open 15 Aug 2026 and close ~17 Nov 2026.
grants.amount: Three years of full tuition + ~$45,500 annual stipend + health insurance allowance
grants.description: Portable 3-year doctoral fellowship for US citizens pursuing science and engineering disciplines of interest to DoD, including computer science, computer/computational engineering, electrical engineering, and mathematics.

grants.name: Vannevar Bush Faculty Fellowship (VBFF)
grants.funder: U.S. Department of Defense (Basic Research Office)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual solicitation; white papers historically due late September. The next white-paper deadline (Class of 2027) is expected ~late September 2026.
grants.region: United States (tenured US faculty, US citizens/permanent residents)
grants.description: DoD's flagship single-investigator basic research award. Networks & AI is a recurring topic area. Open to tenured US faculty with a record of substantial scientific contributions.

grants.name: Qualcomm Innovation Fellowship (North America)
grants.deadlineNote: Regional cycles: North America deadline ~1 December annually (next: ~1 Dec 2026); Europe ~late February; India/APAC ~April. 2026 windows have all closed; 2027 NA cycle expected to open early November 2026.
grants.region: North America, Europe, India, and other APAC regions (invited universities)
grants.description: Mentorship-driven fellowship for PhD (and some Masters) students at invited universities working on novel research in computer science, electrical engineering, and related areas including AI/ML, wireless, computer vision, and systems.

grants.name: Cooperative AI PhD Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: 2026 round closed 16 Nov 2025; next call expected to open around September 2026 for the 2027 cohort.
grants.amount: Up to $40,000/year living stipend plus conference budget and compute support
grants.description: Supports PhD students whose research advances cooperative AI: multi-agent cooperation, AI in social contexts, mechanism design, and the foundations of agentic systems that interact safely with humans and other agents.

grants.name: Paul & Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans
grants.funder: Paul & Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans
grants.deadlineNote: Applicants must be immigrants, the children of immigrants, or DACA recipients, and 30 or younger as of the deadline. Supports any full-time US graduate program including STEM/CS PhDs.
grants.region: United States (graduate study in the US)
grants.amount: Up to $90,000 over two years (stipend + tuition support)
grants.description: Merit-based graduate fellowship for New Americans pursuing full-time graduate or professional degrees in the US. Open to all fields including computer science, AI/ML, and engineering.

grants.name: Schmidt Science Polymaths
grants.deadlineNote: Invitation-only program; institutional nominations are solicited June through mid-August, with full applications typically due in October and decisions in April. The 2027 nomination round is expected to open June 2026.
grants.amount: $500,000 per year for up to 5 years (paid to institution)
grants.description: Supports recently tenured researchers (within 3 years of tenure) with track records of cross-disciplinary work who want to make a substantive methodological or disciplinary shift. Open to mathematics, computer science, natural sciences, and engineering.

grants.name: Snap Research Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; recent rounds have opened in spring with a summer deadline. Confirm current cycle on research.snap.com before applying.
grants.description: Recognizes PhD and MS students conducting exceptional research in computer science areas relevant to Snap including AI/ML, computer vision, computational imaging, computer graphics, HCI, NLP, and AR.

grants.name: ICMR UNNATI - Growth Standards for Indian Children (Phase 2)
grants.deadlineNote: Expression of Interest closes 22 July 2026. Multicentre study; sites must enroll 500-600 children/adolescents in 3-6 months.
grants.description: Multicentre data collection for growth standards for Indian children aged 2-18. Teams need prior experience in large school/community studies on child and adolescent health, standardised anthropometry, and body composition/metabolic health assessment. Open to researchers with regular employment at medical/research institutes, universities, colleges, government/semi-government bodies, and NGOs.

grants.name: India Science Book Fellowship 2026-27
grants.funder: Foundation for Advancing Science and Technology (FAST-India)
grants.deadlineNote: 2026-27 cycle; suggested theme is deep-tech but entries across genres are welcome. Individual or team applications; lead applicant must be based in India.
grants.description: Fellowship for writing a book on science and technology. Includes monetary support, mentorship, publisher introductions, and post-publication promotion via FAST-India.

grants.name: USISTEF - U.S.-India Partnerships in Critical Minerals and Quantum Technology
grants.funder: United States-India Science and Technology Endowment Fund (USISTEF)
grants.deadlineNote: Requires one Indian and one U.S. partner. At least one partner must be entrepreneurial (preferably SME). Academic/government/research partners need an NOC.
grants.description: Bilateral partnerships to develop and commercialise technologies in critical minerals and supply chain resilience, and in quantum technologies. Teams may combine incorporated companies (including start-ups), non-incorporated entities, and academic/government/non-government research institutions.

grants.name: DST-JSPS Indo-Japan Cooperative Science Programme (IJCSP) 2026
grants.funder: Department of Science and Technology (DST), India and Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS)
grants.deadlineNote: Joint Research Projects and Joint Workshops/Seminars. Indian and Japanese PIs must submit the same proposal to their respective agencies. One proposal per PI. Closes 11:59 PM IST.
grants.description: Bilateral scientific collaboration across physical sciences, chemical sciences, life sciences and agriculture, mathematics and computational science, astronomy and earth science, and materials and engineering. Indian PI must be in a regular faculty/scientist position at universities, deemed universities, academic institutes, national R&D laboratories, or private non-profit R&D institutions.

grants.name: MQ Schizophrenia Research Fellowship
grants.funder: MQ Mental Health Research (funded by Nikki Fa Fond via Fondation Philanthropia)
grants.deadlineNote: Expression of Interest due 12 August 2026; full application due 8 December 2026.
grants.description: Fellowship for early-career researchers with 3-7 years of postdoctoral experience working on schizophrenia. Applicants must be based at a university, hospital, or research institution and show a research track record with plans for independence.

grants.name: Ignacio H. de Larramendi Research Grants
grants.deadlineNote: Closes 2:00 PM UTC+2. One principal investigator per project. Open to individuals or institutions in any country.
grants.description: Supports research in health promotion (obesity prevention, physical activity, emotional well-being, emergency response training, bodily-harm assessment, clinical quality and safety, longevity science) and in insurance and social protection (risk management, emerging risks, longevity economics). Open to academic and professional researchers and to research teams led by a researcher, independently or through affiliated universities, hospitals, companies, or research centres.

grants.name: Novo Nordisk Foundation Postdoctoral Fellowships in Plant Science, Agriculture and Food Biotechnology
grants.deadlineNote: Closes 2:00 PM Copenhagen time. PhD obtained within 5 years of the deadline, or PhD student defending before 1 April 2027.
grants.region: Denmark / Global (host in Denmark or abroad)
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowships for early-career researchers in plant science, agricultural science, and food biotechnology. Supports research on sustainable plant production, crop improvement, plant-microbe interactions, and food systems.

grants.name: Lundbeck Foundation LF Experiment
grants.deadlineNote: Open to researchers at all career levels (PhD required). Neuroscience focus.
grants.description: Funds bold, exploratory experiments in neuroscience with the potential to produce results that could not otherwise be obtained. Open to researchers at all career levels.

grants.name: The Brain Prize
grants.deadlineNote: Nomination deadline for outstanding scientists whose work has advanced neuroscience.
grants.description: The world's largest neuroscience prize, awarded annually for outstanding contributions to brain research. Nominations via the Lundbeck Foundation portal.

grants.name: Lundbeck Foundation Start-Up Programme
grants.deadlineNote: Four annual deadlines (Feb 3, Apr 9, Aug 13, Oct 22 in 2026); earlier 2026 rounds have closed. Associate professors receive up to DKK 7M; full professors up to DKK 11M.
grants.description: Supports associate and full professors starting or expanding neuroscience research programmes. Funds staff, equipment, and running costs to establish an independent research direction.

grants.name: Lundbeck Foundation LF Postdocs
grants.deadlineNote: Call opens August 2026; check the Lundbeck Foundation portal for the exact deadline. Applicants must be within 5 years post-PhD and change research environment.
grants.description: Postdoctoral funding for early-career neuroscientists to move to a new research environment (in Denmark or abroad) and establish an independent research direction.

grants.name: SNSF Ambizione
grants.deadlineNote: Closes 5:00 PM CET. Final call before the scheme is replaced. PhD (or medical studies) within 4 years of deadline; physicians up to 9 years post-medical-exam with 3+ years clinical work.
grants.description: Supports early-career researchers who wish to conduct, manage, and lead an independent project at a Swiss higher education institution. Covers the grantee's salary and project funds, or project funds alone if the grantee already holds a salary-bearing position. Swiss nationality, a Swiss degree, or 12+ months of research at a Swiss institution required.

grants.name: Camille Dreyfus Teacher-Scholar Awards
grants.deadlineNote: Announcements early May 2027. Nominations from institutions granting bachelor's degrees or higher in the chemical sciences.
grants.description: Unrestricted research grants for tenure-track faculty within the first six years of their independent academic careers in chemistry, biochemistry, materials chemistry, or chemical engineering, working in PhD-granting departments.

grants.name: Prime Minister's Fellowship for Doctoral Research (PMRF-DR)
grants.funder: Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) and FICCI
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling applications; 100 slots annually. Focus areas identified by industry and national priorities.
grants.amount: INR 70,000/month (years 1-2), INR 75,000/month (year 3), INR 80,000/month (years 4-5) + INR 2 lakh/year contingency
grants.description: Industry-oriented doctoral fellowship in science, technology, engineering, agriculture, and medicine at reputed Indian technology and research institutions. Distinct from the standard PMRF; the Direct Route is administered by FICCI on behalf of ANRF and emphasises industry-relevant PhD research.

grants.name: Haryana Research and Development Scheme (2026-27)
grants.funder: Directorate of Higher Education, Government of Haryana
grants.deadlineNote: Call for the 2026-27 financial year is open. Deadline announced via press advertisement on the directorate portal; submissions online via the Head of Institution.
grants.amount: Up to INR 50 lakh per project
grants.description: State R&D grant for regular faculty at recognised Higher Education Institutions in Haryana. Prefers problem-oriented S&T research aimed at socio-economic development of the state - rural livelihoods, local resources, artisan skills, and need-based applied R&D.

grants.name: Leverhulme Trust Early Career Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; 2026 round closed 19 February 2026. Next Trust deadline expected February 2027; institutional expressions of interest typically close late November-December.
grants.amount: Salary contribution up to GBP 56,000 (year 1); 50% of salary up to GBP 28,000 in years 2-3
grants.description: ~145 fellowships annually across all disciplines including sciences, engineering, and mathematics. Trust contributes salary; host institution provides the balance. Applicants must be within 4 years of PhD (with allowances). Fellowships commence between September and May.

grants.name: Royal Society Newton International Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; 2026 round closed 11 March 2026. Next call typically opens mid-January with an early-March deadline; institutional internal deadlines run earlier.
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowships for early-career, non-UK-based researchers in the natural, physical and social sciences to conduct research at UK host institutions. Eligibility rules and sponsor limits changed from the 2026 round.

grants.name: Alexander von Humboldt Research Fellowship for Postdoctoral Researchers
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling with three selection meetings per year (March, July, November). Application windows open 15 March, 15 July, and 15 November each year; the 800-application cap can close a window early.
grants.description: Long-term Humboldt fellowship for researchers from any country and any discipline (natural sciences, engineering, humanities) to conduct long-term research in Germany hosted by a collaborator of their choice. Experienced Researcher variant available for those up to 12 years post-PhD.

grants.name: NASA Postdoctoral Program (NPP)
grants.deadlineNote: Three annual deadlines: 1 March, 1 July, and 1 November. Closes at 6:00 PM US ET. Three letters of recommendation and transcripts (for Postdoctoral Fellow tier) must arrive by the deadline.
grants.region: United States (host NASA centres); applicants US and international
grants.amount: USD 82,500+ stipend (varies by centre) + relocation and health allowances
grants.description: Fellowships hosted at NASA centres to advance NASA's missions across Earth science, planetary science, heliophysics, astrophysics, biological and physical sciences, aeronautics, human exploration, space operations, space technology, and astrobiology. PhD in hand before start (may apply while finishing).

grants.name: DFG Emmy Noether Programme
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling submissions; proposals accepted anytime. Occasional themed calls (e.g. Methods in AI, June 2026) run alongside.
grants.description: Enables exceptionally qualified early-career researchers to qualify for a professorship by leading an independent junior research group. Open to postdocs and junior professors with temporary contracts across all disciplines.

grants.name: DFG Walter Benjamin Programme
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling submissions; proposals accepted anytime.
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowship enabling researchers to independently conduct a project at a research institution of their choice in Germany or abroad. Return module supports subsequent re-integration into the German research system.

grants.name: Cottrell Scholar Award
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle closes 1 July; portal opens ~April. 2027 cycle closed 1 July 2026 (2027 awards announced Feb 2027). Next application window: April-July 2027 for the 2028 cohort. Eligibility keyed to first faculty appointment year.
grants.description: Awards to third-year tenure-track faculty in chemistry, physics, and astronomy for integrated programmes of research and academic leadership. Prior awardees are eligible for follow-on SEED, STAR, and IMPACT awards.

grants.name: IndiaAI Fellowship (PhD)
grants.funder: IndiaAI Mission, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; 2026 round closed 28 February 2026. 100 PhD fellowships per year. Next call expected early 2027. Also supports UG (INR 1 lakh) and PG (M.Tech) tiers.
grants.amount: INR 70,000/month (years 1-2), INR 75,000/month (year 3), INR 80,000/month (years 4-5) + INR 2 lakh/year research grant
grants.description: PhD fellowship for full-time scholars researching Artificial Intelligence at India's top NIRF-ranked institutions. Structured to bridge theoretical AI research and practical applications, aligned with IndiaAI Mission priorities.

grants.name: FENS/IBRO-PERC Exchange Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: For applicants based in Europe. Supports laboratory visits to other European neuroscience labs.
grants.description: Exchange fellowship supporting PhD students and early-career postdoctoral researchers in neuroscience to visit host laboratories elsewhere in Europe for training and collaboration. Companion to the global IBRO Exchange Fellowships.

grants.name: Wadhwani AI - AI for All Research Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling; apply via email to careers@wadhwaniai.org with CV and short statement of interest. Technical assessment and interview follow.
grants.description: Research fellowship for recent or graduating undergraduate (BTech/BE/BS) and Master's (MTech/ME/MS) students to work on AI systems for societal-scale problems in under-resourced settings - healthcare, agriculture, education, and public infrastructure - across India and globally.

grants.name: La Caixa Postdoctoral Junior Leader Fellowships - Retaining
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; 2026 Retaining call closed 25 September 2025. Next call expected mid-2026 for 2027. 20 fellowships per year.
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowship to carry out a research project at any university or research centre in Spain or Portugal. STEM disciplines. Candidates must have been awarded a PhD 2-7 years before the deadline. Companion Incoming call funds work at Severo Ochoa / Maria de Maeztu / Carlos III / FCT-accredited centres.

grants.name: La Caixa Postdoctoral Junior Leader Fellowships - Incoming
grants.deadlineNote: Annual Incoming cycle; deadline typically early February. 20 fellowships per year.
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowship to conduct research at Spanish centres accredited by the Severo Ochoa, Maria de Maeztu, or Carlos III Health Institute excellence seals, or Portuguese centres accredited by FCT. STEM disciplines. Candidates must have PhD awarded 2-7 years before the deadline.

grants.name: CZI Accelerating Biological Sciences via AI - GPU Compute Allocations
grants.deadlineNote: 2026 cycle closed 18 June 2026. Program provides access to CZI's 1,024 NVIDIA H100 DGX SuperPOD cluster; next RFA expected 2027.
grants.amount: GPU-hours on CZI's H100 cluster (no cash grant)
grants.description: RFA for large-scale AI/ML models in biological sciences that cannot be built with conventional university compute. Provides allocations on CZI's NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD (1,024 H100 GPUs) with VAST fast storage.

grants.name: IBRO Exchange Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: Annual global cycle; 2026 round closed 15 April 2026 with 20 fellows selected. Next call expected early 2027.
grants.description: Fellowships for PhD students and early-career postdoctoral neuroscientists to conduct laboratory visits internationally for training, collaboration, and access to techniques. Complementary regional variants include the FENS/IBRO-PERC exchange for European-based applicants.

grants.name: RGSTC Assistance to Innovative S&T Applications
grants.funder: Rajiv Gandhi Science & Technology Commission, Government of Maharashtra
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling; proposals invited through host institutions in Maharashtra. Also runs the CPIITA scheme (Collaborative Projects between Institutions and Industries for Technology Development/Adoption).
grants.description: State S&T grants for innovative applications of science and technology aimed at socio-economic development of Maharashtra. Open to R&D institutions, universities, and colleges in the state. CPIITA scheme supports academia-industry collaborative technology development/adoption.
